name: Universal Regex & Check-Character Validator — IDs, codes & patterns
description: Live, as-you-type validation for Text/Notes fields with a structure: participant and specimen IDs minted with check characters (ISO 7064, Damm, Verhoeff, Luhn — single fields or pooled several-IDs-in-one-box fields), or any regex-shaped code with progressive what's-still-missing guidance while typing. Enforcement happens in the BROWSER, per rule: message, confirm, or block the form/survey save. A post-save hook additionally logs invalid values to the module log as a best-effort audit wherever REDCap fires redcap_save_record — verify on your own instance whether that covers API and Data Import writes before relying on it (see the documentation). Three ways to configure: the settings dialog (one rule, many fields), fast field-name entry, or @UVALIDATE field annotations for bulk setup from the data dictionary.

authors.name: CHPR
authors.institution: Bamenda Center for Health Promotion and Research (CHPR)

action-tags.description: Validate this Text or Notes field with the Universal Regex & Check-Character Validator, from the Online Designer or the data dictionary CSV instead of the module's Configure dialog. Forms: @UVALIDATE (default ISO 7064 Mod 37,36 check), @UVALIDATE=verhoeff (pick an algorithm), or a full-rule JSON such as @UVALIDATE={"algorithm":"none","pattern":"FC[0-9]{4}","blockSave":"hard"} or @UVALIDATE={"type":"pooled","expectedIds":3}. Algorithm names accept shorthands so you need not spell out the full name: e.g. @UVALIDATE=3736 (or 37,36, mod37_36) = ISO 7064 Mod 37,36, 9710 = Mod 97,10, 112 = Mod 11,2, letters1/letters2, mod10 = Luhn, gs1 = GS1 Mod-10, aba = ABA Mod-10, mrz = ICAO MRZ Mod-10, isbn = Weighted Mod-11, and regex (or format) = no check character. JSON keys: type, algorithm, source, pattern, strip, keepChars, idLengths, idMinLen, idMaxLen, expectedIds, blockSave, when, note. The optional when key makes the rule conditional — it validates only while a REDCap-style condition is true, e.g. @UVALIDATE={"algorithm":"verhoeff","when":"[specimen_type]='2'"} (supports [field] and [checkbox(code)] refs, = <> != > < >= <=, and/or/not, parentheses; a false condition skips the rule, it does NOT erase the value). One field may carry SEVERAL tags when each has a different when (plus at most one without, as the fallback) — branched validation. suggestFix (true/false, default false) opts in to the "should end in X" check-character hint. Fields with identical tags are grouped into one rule; a malformed tag shows a configuration error.

project-settings.name:
Universal Regex & Check-Character Validator
Add ONE rule per kind of validation — a rule can cover any number of fields. Three ways to attach fields to a rule: the field picker (click its + to add more fields), the fast-entry box (type field names separated by commas), or skip this dialog entirely and tag fields with @UVALIDATE in the Online Designer's Action Tags box / the data dictionary CSV (bulk setup — see the documentation). Leave a rule's advanced boxes blank to use the defaults.

How to log invalid values caught by the post-save audit. Every mode except 'off' logs field, instrument, event and instance; the modes differ in how the invalid VALUE and the RECORD ID are stored. 'Hashed' is a keyed, project-scoped HMAC (module-held secret) — that is pseudonymization, not anonymity: treat the module log as identifying data for access and retention purposes.

choices.name: Keyed hash of the value + raw record ID (default — correlates repeats within this project without storing the value; the record ID stays readable so staff can fix the record)
Strict: no value, record ID as keyed hash (minimal-identifier mode for sites where record IDs are themselves identifying)
Raw value + raw record ID (only if your data-governance policy allows identifiers in module logs)
Do not log detections at all (audit ERRORS are still logged, without any record identifier)

project-settings.name: Debug: include exception messages in audit-error log entries. Leave OFF in production — exception text can quote data values.

sub_settings.name: Rule label (optional — your own name for this rule, e.g. 'Specimen IDs' or 'Legacy screening codes')

choices.name: Pooled (several IDs jammed into one field)

sub_settings.name: Field(s) this rule validates — click + to add more fields to this same rule
Fast entry (optional): more field names for this rule, separated by commas or spaces. Merged with the pickers above; unknown names show a configuration error.

Only validate when (optional): a REDCap-style condition such as [specimen_type]='2' or [consent(1)]='1' and [site]<>'9'. Supports [field] and [checkbox(code)] references, = <> != > < >= <=, and/or/not, and parentheses; functions, [event][field] prefixes and smart variables are not supported. Fields on the same page are read live; fields on other instruments use their saved values. While the condition is false the rule is skipped entirely — the value is NOT erased (unlike field branching). Several rules may cover the SAME field when each has a different condition (plus at most one without, as the fallback) — the rule whose condition is true validates the field.
Check-character method — must match how the IDs were minted. Validating a plain format/code with no check character? Pick 'No check character' and set the format pattern below.

choices.name: ISO 7064 Mod 11,2 — digits, check 0-9 or X
ABA Mod-10 — digits, 1 char (US routing style)
ICAO MRZ Mod-10 — digits ONLY, 1 char (7,3,1 passport scheme; not for letter-bearing MRZ fields)
Weighted Mod-11 — digits, 1 char incl. X (ISBN-10 style; full strength only up to 9 digits — prefer Mod 11,2 for longer IDs)

sub_settings.name: Suggest the correct final check character on a mismatch (OFF by default — a visible "should end in X" hint can entice staff to force-fit a mistyped ID instead of re-scanning it; enable only where that risk is acceptable)

Optional format pattern (regex for ONE ID; printable ASCII; tested against the UPPERCASED, dash-unified value). Required if method is 'No check character'. JavaScript regex syntax; catastrophic patterns — nested quantifiers, repeated ambiguous groups, and overlapping unbounded quantifiers such as (a+)+, (a|aa)+, or .*.* — are rejected at save time.
Separators to ignore before checking (default: - / space _ | \ )
Pooled only: extra characters to keep when splitting a pooled field (rarely needed; printable ASCII, up to 64)
Pooled only: exact ID length(s), comma-separated (e.g. 10 or 10, 12; up to 32 values, each up to 64). Overrides the min/max range below.
Pooled only: minimum ID length (default 8). Used only if exact lengths are blank.
Pooled only: maximum ID length (default 14; must be less than 2× the minimum; up to 64).
Pooled only: expected number of IDs in the field (optional; warns if different). Positive whole number up to 9999.
On an invalid value (all three are BROWSER behaviors; API and import writes cannot be blocked — they are only logged per the audit-log setting above)

choices.name: Advisory — warn and ask before the browser saves
Compulsory — block the browser form/survey save until fixed (never blocks API/import writes; skipped on read-only fields)
